scope.family: encoded-mouth stabilizer bridge states dressed by a complete logical-CZ interaction graph on right encoded mouths
scope.interaction: logical CZ between right encoded mouths
scope.result_type: positive interacting bridge theorem plus static-state transition no-go

core_claim: A non-product logical-CZ-dressed encoded bridge state determines its observer algebra and mouth map while low-order entropy remains blind; state-derived decoding restores transfer, wrong-mouth and mouth-blind controls fail, and static state data alone provably do not determine a north/south recovery transition.

theorem_style_claims.claim: The logical-CZ-dressed encoded bridge is non-product; full-block MI infers the mouth map; the inferred interaction graph dresses the observer algebra; state-derived decoding restores exact transfer.
Aligned and twisted interacting witnesses match through physical entropy order d=3, with first split at d+1=4.
The same static state signature is compatible with opposite north- and south-favored recovery channel completions, so static state data alone do not determine the screen transition.
When the screen isometry is included as dynamics, success probabilities are read from induced channels and the equal-area entropy analogue matches recovery ordering.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.scope: aligned and twisted two-mouth logical-CZ-dressed encoded resources
all pairings for m<=3 under complete logical-CZ interaction
same static state signature with opposite screen-channel completions
bounded_exhaustive_evidence.minimal_inserted_ingredient: screen dynamics/isometry or equivalent recovery rule

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 15 interacting bridge theorem certificate.
Regression check for the Goal 15 certificate.

diagnostic_hierarchy.coarse_entropy_mincut: insufficient for the interacting mouth map
diagnostic_hierarchy.inter_bridge_mi_excess: infers the logical-CZ interaction graph in the declared family
diagnostic_hierarchy.dressed_observer_algebra: recovered by conjugating right logical algebra through the inferred interaction graph
diagnostic_hierarchy.screen_isometry: minimal dynamics needed for the recovery/area transition

known_vs_new.known_derived: Standard stabilizer codes, logical CZ conjugation, stabilizer entropy, mutual information, Bell teleportation, Choi/Kraus channel checks, and erasure-channel recovery.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages the first non-product finite bridge certificate in this sequence and identifies a sharp static-state-only obstruction for recovery/area transitions.

limitations: This is an exact finite stabilizer/QEC theorem and dense-channel certificate. It is not continuum ER=EPR, de Sitter physics, dS/CFT, or a chaotic many-body simulation. The positive theorem is for logical-CZ-dressed encoded-mouth resources; the transition theorem is a no-go unless screen dynamics are supplied.
next_frontier: Replace the logical-CZ dressing and erasure screen completion with a single interacting finite circuit whose screen isometry is inseparable from the bridge dynamics.
